statements.pure: For single-attractor functional graph with attractor fixed point, nilpotency index of pure transient operator N_pure (edges to attractor dropped to 0) equals graph height H
statements.augmented: Nilpotency index of augmented operator N_aug (transient plus sink, edges to cycle mapped to sink) equals H+1
statements.general: For general functional graph, pure nilpotency = max height across components, augmented = max height +1

verification.method: random_functional_graph with exact cycle detection and quotient operator as in quotient_estimate

definition.P: averaging projection onto partition blocks: P[i,j]=1/|B| if i,j in same block

interpretation.Dpull=0: partition is congruence: x~y => T(x)~T(y), forward dynamics respects coarse-graining
interpretation.Dpush=0: partition is balanced: indegree distribution uniform w.r.t blocks, backward reconstruction preserves coarse measure
interpretation.asymmetry: Delta = ||Dpull|| - ||Dpush|| measures irreversibility of information transport

finite_example_T0.interpretation: pullback congruence holds (deterministic coarse dynamics), pushforward fails (non-uniform branching)

chain_0_to_1.interpretation: coarse partition collapses fixed point with transient, forward still deterministic, backward loses mass conservation
